results.description: Submit intake request without signature or timestamp
results.reason: Blocked — missing required signature and timestamp fields

results.description: Sign intent via /sign-intent, then submit to /intake with valid signature
results.reason: Executed successfully — signature verified, receipt hash and ledger index assigned

results.description: Sign intent, execute once, then replay the exact same signed payload
results.actual_result: First: HTTP 200 (success); Replay: HTTP 409 (Replay blocked); Expired: HTTP 409 (Replay blocked)
results.reason: Single-use enforcement confirmed. First use succeeded, replay blocked (HTTP 409 — 409 Replay blocked), expired replay also blocked (HTTP 409).

results.description: Sign intent A, submit with tampered intent B using same signature
results.actual_result: HTTP 401: unauthorized — Signature verification failedIntent was not signed by authorized key
results.reason: Blocked — signature verification failed because signed payload doesn't match submitted payload

results.description: Execute an approved intent once, then verify the approval cannot be reused (consumed/revoked)
results.actual_result: First use: HTTP 200 (success); Reuse attempt: HTTP 409 (Replay blocked)
results.reason: Approval consumed after single use (HTTP 409 on reuse). Combined with 300s time-window expiry, approvals are structurally revoked after use or after timeout — whichever comes first.

results.description: Call executor endpoints (/tools/*, /intent) directly without valid Bearer token
results.actual_result: No auth→/tools/call_anthropic: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); No auth→/intent: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); No auth→/tools/send_email: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED); Fake token→/tools/call_anthropic: HTTP 403 (BLOCKED)
results.reason: All direct executor calls blocked — Bearer token required and validated

results.description: Submit intake with garbage, empty, and foreign-key signatures
results.actual_result: Garbage sig: HTTP 401 (BLOCKED); Empty sig: HTTP 401 (BLOCKED); Foreign sig: HTTP 500 (BLOCKED)
results.reason: All invalid signatures rejected — ECDSA verification catches any non-matching signature

results.description: Verify that execution is blocked when the ledger service is unavailable
results.actual_result: Server-side simulation confirmed by Replit Agent: when the nonce registry (backed by the same SQLite DB as the ledger) raises a RuntimeError, the gateway catches it and returns HTTP 503 'Nonce registry unavailable — execution blocked'. The AI call is never reached.
results.reason: Fail-closed confirmed via server-side simulation. Any DB/ledger error blocks execution with HTTP 503 before the AI model call. Logged as REJECT | reason=nonce_check_error.

results.description: Verify that execution is blocked when the signature verification service is unavailable
results.actual_result: Server-side simulation confirmed by Replit Agent: signature verification is wrapped in try/except with fail-closed behavior. If the public key is missing (RIO_PUBLIC_KEY unset), _load_verifying_key() returns None and all /intake requests are rejected. Any exception during ECDSA verification is caught and blocks execution.
results.reason: Fail-closed confirmed via server-side simulation. Missing or broken signature verification service blocks all execution. No approval can be forged or bypassed.

results.description: Submit the same signed intent twice in rapid succession
results.actual_result: Exec1: HTTP 200 status=success ledger=3; Exec2: HTTP 409 status=Replay blocked
results.reason: Duplicate execution blocked (HTTP 409 — 409 Replay blocked). Each signed approval executes exactly once.
